ab: Iraay i loma’ ko fofo haw?
en: Is grandmother at home?

ab: Iraay, mafoti’ay cira i kafoti’an.
en: Yes, she’s sleeping in her room.

ab: Iraay i loma’ ko fofo haw?
en: Is grandmother at home?

ab: Awaay, mahadakay cira, dadaya nga, minokayay.
ch: B:不在，她剛出去了，要晚上才回家。
en: No, she’s not. She just went out and will be back in the evening.

ab: Iraay i loma’ ko akong haw?
en: Is grandfather at home?

ab: Iraay i kafekang o sakarosa a tingroh, kilimen cira.
ch: B:他在二樓，你上去找他吧。
en: He’s upstairs. You can go up and find him.

ab: Iraay ko tamdaw i loma’ haw?
en: Is anyone at home?

ab: Ci Kacaw ha! Halafin to cowa ka’araw kiso. Nga’ayay ho ko miso?
ch: B:是Kacaw阿，很久沒有看到你了，你好嗎?
en: It’s Kacaw. I haven’t seen you in a while! How have you been?

ab: Nga’ayay! Malaholol ho kita sa kako.
en: I’m doing great! I came to chat with you.

ab: Icowaay ko ama iso?
en: Where is your father?

ab: Miciyanay cira to dateng i caka, paiyoyen ako cira.
ch: B:他在廚房炒菜，我去叫他。
en: He’s in the kitchen cooking. Let me go call him.

ab: Talacowaay ci Panay?
en: Where did Panay go?

ab: Micepetay cira to koko’ i pakoko’an.
en: She went to the chicken coop to catch chickens.

ab: Akong, tayniay to kami.
en: Grandfather, we’re here.

ab: Awaay i loma’ ko akong namo, itiraay i omah cira. i loma’ sa ho kamo a mitala ciraan.
ch: B:你們的爺爺不在家，他在田裡，你們在家等他回來吧。
en: Your grandfather is not at home. He’s in the field. Wait for him to come back.

ab: Faki, iraay to kiso!
en: Uncle, you’re here!

ab: Halafin to cowa ka’araw kiso, ’akawangay to kiso.
en: I haven’t seen you in a while. You’ve grown taller.

ab: Mahemek ko faloco’ to katayni iso.
en: It’s so good to see you.

ab: O papafelien kamo tona onglay, pingsyangen.
ch: B:這是給你們的鳳梨，要放到冰箱裡。
en: Here’s a pineapple for you. Put it in the refrigerator.

ab: Halafin sa, awa ho a tayni, pohayen ho ako cangra a mapolong a malahok.
ch: A:這麼久沒來，我叫大家一起來吃飯。
en: It’s been a while. Let’s have a meal together.

ab: Hapimaan, ocya sa ko nananomen ako, itira kita i palafangan a malaholoholol.
ch: B:不用麻煩了，我喝茶就好，我們來客廳聊天。
en: No need to bother. I’ll just have tea. Let’s sit in the living room and chat.

ab: Ahowiday to pihamham iso. Minokay to kako.
ch: A:謝謝你的招待，我要回去了。
en: Thank you for your hospitality. I have to go now.

ab: Maherekay to a mili’opih koni koko’, panokayen cedeng han iso a komaen.
ch: B:這隻雞剛殺好，你帶回去慢慢吃。
en: This chicken was just freshly killed. Take it home and enjoy it slowly.
